summary: This week in AI, major developments include Anthropic's $1.5B copyright settlement, advancements in firefighting drones, and Google's new AI chip for Gemini. OpenAI and Anthropic models are being tested by US public health agencies, while China's AI companies are ramping up pressure on Silicon Valley.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is raising concerns about job displacement, bias, and copyright infringement, highlighting the need for responsible AI development and regulation.
highlights: Anthropic's landmark $1.5B copyright settlement sets a precedent for AI model training
Google is working on a new AI chip to make Gemini models more efficient
China's AI companies are challenging US dominance with new models and technologies

articles.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
articles.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

articles.title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
articles.description: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.

articles.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
articles.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

articles.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
articles.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.

articles.title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
articles.description:
Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe

articles.title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
articles.description: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app.

articles.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
articles.description: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

articles.title: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
articles.description: China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthr

articles.title: US public health agencies to test OpenAI and Anthropic AI models
articles.description: Public health departments across the United States will test generative AI tools under a new programme involving the Coalition for Health AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Accenture. The

articles.title: Kimi K3 open-weight model: China’s biggest AI is a bet on memory, not compute
articles.description: Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 open-weight model has been read almost entirely through its parameter count since it launchedon July 16. At 2.8 trillion parameters, it is the largest open-we

articles.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
articles.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

articles.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
articles.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open-weight AI models for coding.
